title: Board Meetings: Public Participation
description: Community members, parents, and students have the right to speak at Conroe ISD board meetings, and this policy explains how that process works. It covers how people can sign up to address the board, how much time speakers are given, and what topics are appropriate to raise in a public session. Understanding these guidelines helps residents make their voices heard on issues that affect local schools.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Board Policy BED (Local) adds a new "Public Comment" section that prioritizes parents of currently enrolled students to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of public board meetings, and replaces the word "complaints" with "grievances" throughout the Complaints/Grievances and Concerns section.
changes_added: Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.
timeline_local.notes: The cover page recommends adoption (not a first reading). Red underlined text identified two distinct change areas: (1) a new "Public Comment" paragraph giving enrolled students' parents priority speaking rights, and (2) the renaming of "Complaints" to "Grievances" in the section heading and all three bullet points. Black text (all other policy language) was excluded as unchanged.

timeline_local.summary: This update to BED (Local) adds a new Public Comment provision giving parents of currently enrolled students priority to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting, and renames the "Complaints" section heading and bullet list references to "Grievances" throughout.
changes_added: Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.

### First Reading / Discussion Only Policies (No Votes):
- **BED** — Board Meeting Public Participation
- **DF** — Termination of Employment
- **DGBA** — Employee Complaints and Grievances
- **EA** — Instructional Goals and Objectives
- **EMBB** — Teaching About Controversial Issues (added definition)
- **FA** — Parents Rights/Responsibilities (legislative compliance; board meetings must be held outside regular business hours, defined as 8–5; applies to regular and special meetings but not committee meetings)
- **FFB** — Threat Assessment (new policy — district was not previously compliant; brought forward by Trustee Horton; moved from incorrect placement to first reading)
- **FL** — Student Records (legislative)
- **FM** — Student Activities/Extracurricular
- **FNG** — Student/Parent Complaints changed to Grievances (legislative)
- **GF** — Public Complaints/Communications

timeline_local.notes: Cover page recommendation states the Board should "adopt" the updated policy, indicating this is an adoption action. Red underlined text clearly identifies the new Public Comment paragraph for parents of enrolled students. Red strikethrough text clearly shows replacement of the word "Complaints" with "Grievances" in the section heading and all three bullet points. All other policy text is unchanged black text and was excluded.

description_local: This policy governs how school board meetings are conducted, including the rules for scheduling, running, and participating in official board meetings. It applies to school board members, district staff, and members of the public who wish to attend or speak at meetings. For parents and community members, this policy is especially important because it outlines their right to address the board during public comment — and a recent update gives parents of currently enrolled students priority when signing up to speak.
